name: codex-git-ask-downgraded-deny
doc: Codex parses but NEVER honors `permissionDecision:"ask"` (deny-only) — an `ask` would silently fail OPEN (the tool runs). respond.ts:44-49 downgrades every `ask` to an explicit `deny` on the codex envelope, prefixed `[downgraded from ask — Codex has no interactive approval]` so the honest limit is visible. A plain `git push` (GIT_ASK, an `ask` on Claude Code) therefore lands as a DENY on Codex, not a silent allow. Contrast: scenario 11 (claude-code) asserts the SAME command resolves to `permissionDecision:"ask"` — same policy, harness-correct envelope. Real output verified 2026-07-06 against `bun src/cli/bin.ts hook codex core`.

steps.doc: `git push origin main` matches GIT_ASK (patterns.ts:15) → `ask` prompt. On Codex the `ask` is downgraded to `deny` (respond.ts:47-48). Assert the decision is `deny` (NOT `ask`) — the proof Codex never fails open on a risky-but-not-destructive op.
Same command, asserting the honest-limit prefix is present so the downgrade is visible to the user/agent, not a silent policy swap.
